page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance features a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB per core. The processor architecture includes a 36 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, achieving top-tier results in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation using Clang, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong tiers, respectively.

The instance is equipped with a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-bound and EBS-intensive workloads. Given its high multi-core efficiency, top-tier database operations performance, and high static web serving throughput, the c8gn.24xlarge is suited for high-throughput web serving, distributed database nodes, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks. It is highly optimized for network-intensive operations, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This hardware profile is well-suited for high-throughput workloads, including software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.

tagline: A high-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering exceptional multi-core performance and high-bandwidth networking for demanding enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS

Suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
